meaning: adj.鍛抽亾宸殑,涓嶅ソ鍚冪殑
examples: Only then did I learn the unpalatable truth.
It was only then that I began to learn the unpalatable truth about John.
But before we try to emulate geniuses or encourage our children to do so we should note that some of the things we learn from them may prove unpalatable.
If Fiat does not sell the car division, it is faced with an unpalatable choice.
And those conditions are what have proven so unpalatable to the Italians -- austerity and reform.
